descriptions.DELETE /live-streams/{liveStreamId}/thum: Send the unique identifier for a live stream to delete it from the system.
descriptions.DELETE /players/{playerId}: Delete a player if you no longer need it.
descriptions.DELETE /videos/{videoId}: If you do not need a video any longer, you can send a request to delete it.
descriptions.DELETE /videos/{videoId}/captions/{langu: Delete a caption in a specific language by providing the video ID for the video you want to delete the caption from and the language the caption is in.
descriptions.DELETE /webhooks/{webhookId}: This endpoint will delete the indicated webhook.
descriptions.GET /analytics/sessions/{sessionId}/even: Useful to track and measure video's engagement.
descriptions.GET /analytics/videos/{videoId}: Retrieve all available user sessions for a specific video.
descriptions.GET /live-streams: With no parameters added to the url, this will return all livestreams.
descriptions.GET /live-streams/{liveStreamId}: Supply a LivestreamId, and you'll get all the details for streaming into, and watching the livestream.
descriptions.GET /players: Retrieve a list of all the players you created, as well as details about each one.
descriptions.GET /players/{playerId}: Use a player ID to retrieve details about the player and display it for viewers.
descriptions.GET /upload-tokens: A delegated token is used to allow secure uploads without exposing your API key.
descriptions.GET /upload-tokens/{uploadToken}: You can retrieve details about a specific upload token if you have the unique identifier for the upload token.
descriptions.GET /videos: Requests to this endpoint return a list of your videos (with all their details).
descriptions.GET /videos/{videoId}: This call provides the same JSON information provided on video creation.
descriptions.GET /videos/{videoId}/captions: Retrieve a list of available captions for the videoId you provide.
descriptions.GET /videos/{videoId}/captions/{language: Display a caption for a video in a specific language.
descriptions.GET /videos/{videoId}/chapters: Retrieve a list of all chapters for a specified video.
descriptions.GET /videos/{videoId}/chapters/{language: Chapters help your viewers find the sections of the video they are most interested in viewing.
descriptions.GET /videos/{videoId}/status: This API provides upload status & encoding status to determine when the video is uploaded or ready to playback.
descriptions.GET /webhooks: Requests to this endpoint return a list of your webhooks (with all their details).
descriptions.GET /webhooks/{webhookId}: This call provides the same JSON information provided on Webjhook creation.
descriptions.PATCH /live-streams/{liveStreamId}: Use this endpoint to update the player, or to turn recording on/off (saving a copy of the livestream).
descriptions.PATCH /players/{playerId}: Use a player ID to update specific details for a player.
descriptions.PATCH /videos/{videoId}: Use this endpoint to update the parameters associated with your video.
descriptions.PATCH /videos/{videoId}/captions/{langua: To have the captions on automatically, use this PATCH to set default: true.
descriptions.PATCH /videos/{videoId}/thumbnail: Pick a thumbnail from the given time code.
descriptions.POST /auth/api-key: To get started, submit your API key in the body of your request.
descriptions.POST /auth/refresh: Use the refresh endpoint with the refresh token you received when you first authenticated using the api-key endpoint.
descriptions.POST /live-streams: A live stream will give you the 'connection point' to RTMP your video stream to api.video.
descriptions.POST /live-streams/{liveStreamId}/thumbn: Upload an image to use as a backdrop for your livestream.
descriptions.POST /players: Create a player for your video, and customise it.
descriptions.POST /players/{playerId}/logo: The uploaded image maximum size should be 200x100 and its weight should be 200KB.
descriptions.POST /upload: When given a token, anyone can upload a file to the URI `https://ws.api.video/upload?token=<tokenId>`.
descriptions.POST /upload-tokens: Use this endpoint to generate an upload token.
descriptions.POST /videos: To create a video, you create its container&parameters first, before adding the video file (exception - when using an existing HTTP source).
descriptions.POST /videos/{videoId}/captions/{languag: Upload a VTT file to add captions to your video.
descriptions.POST /videos/{videoId}/chapters/{languag: Chapters help break the video into sections.
descriptions.POST /videos/{videoId}/source: To upload a video to the videoId you created.
descriptions.POST /videos/{videoId}/thumbnail: The thumbnail is the poster that appears in the player window before video playback begins.
descriptions.POST /webhooks: Webhooks can push notifications to your server, rather than polling api.video for changes.

notes.DELETE /live-streams/{liveStreamId}/thum: Audit: label=delete confirmed. Summary says 'Delete a thumbnail'. Heuristic misclassifies as generic because sub-resource path without own ID parameter — L1 doesn't recognize DELETE on /resource/{id}/sub-resource as delete.
notes.DELETE /players/{playerId}/logo: Audit: label=delete confirmed. Summary says 'Delete logo'. Same sub-resource delete pattern as thumbnail above.
notes.GET /account: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. Singleton — summary says 'Show account'. No collection path exists. Heuristic misclassifies as list because no path parameter.
notes.GET /analytics/live-streams/{liveStreamI: Audit: label=list confirmed. Summary says 'List live stream player sessions'. Despite {liveStreamId} in path suggesting fetch, this returns a list of analytics sessions for that stream, not the stream itself.
notes.GET /analytics/videos/{videoId}: Audit: label=list confirmed. Summary says 'List video player sessions'. Same analytics pattern — {videoId} scopes the list, not identifies a single resource.
notes.GET /videos/{videoId}/status: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. Singleton sub-resource — summary says 'Show video status'. Returns a single status object for the video.
notes.PATCH /videos/{videoId}/thumbnail: Pick a thumbnail (action), not standard update
notes.POST /auth/api-key: Revised: generic→create. Creates an access token resource
notes.POST /live-streams/{liveStreamId}/thumbn: Revised: generic→create. Upload creates a thumbnail sub-resource
notes.POST /players/{playerId}/logo: Revised: generic→create. Upload creates a logo sub-resource
notes.POST /upload: Revised: generic→create. Upload creates a video resource
notes.POST /videos/{videoId}/captions/{languag: File upload (caption file), not standard create
notes.POST /videos/{videoId}/chapters/{languag: File upload (chapter file), not standard create
notes.POST /videos/{videoId}/source: Revised: generic→create. Upload creates a video source sub-resource
notes.POST /videos/{videoId}/thumbnail: Revised: generic→create. Upload creates a thumbnail sub-resource
